scenario: Aurora fintech upgrades 30 microservices to Camunda 8.8 and the platform team audits every application.yaml file. Several services still set camunda.client.cluster-id carried over from earlier minor versions, and the team must move the value to the documented replacement property listed in the Spring Boot starter reference before removing the legacy alias.
question: Which property replaces the legacy camunda.client.cluster-id in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id to carry the cluster identifier under the cloud configuration subtree of the client
Set camunda.client.zeebe.cluster-id to carry the cluster identifier under the zeebe configuration subtree of the client
Set camunda.client.auth.cluster-id to carry the cluster identifier under the auth configuration subtree of the client
Set camunda.client.identity.cluster-id to carry the cluster identifier under the identity configuration subtree of the client

a.text: Correct. The reference table lists camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.cluster-id property in Camunda 8.8, alongside camunda.client.cloud.region for the region alias.

b.text: Incorrect. The zeebe subtree carries client-connection aliases such as base-url and grpc-address, not the cluster identifier; the documented cluster-id replacement lives under camunda.client.cloud.

c.text: Incorrect. The auth subtree holds OIDC fields such as audience and scope; the cluster identifier replacement is camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id, not an auth property.

d.text: Incorrect. The identity subtree is itself listed as deprecated with replacements under auth; the cluster-id alias is replaced specifically by camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id.

explanation: The Spring Boot starter properties reference groups camunda.client.cluster-id under deprecated properties with replacement camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id. The companion property camunda.client.region similarly moves under camunda.client.cloud.region in Camunda 8.8.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
